steps.title: Identify what the equation must represent
steps.content: The container starts with 24 mL. Water drips in at a constant rate (one 0.03 mL drop every 3 seconds), so $v$ is a linear function of $t$ with initial value 24 and a positive slope equal to the drip rate.

steps.title: Calculate the drip rate
steps.content: Rate = $\frac{0.03 \text{ mL}}{3 \text{ s}} = 0.01$ mL per second. After $t$ seconds the added volume is $0.01t$ mL, giving $v = 0.01t + 24$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $v = 0.01t + 24$: Rate 0.01 mL/s, starting at 24 mL. Matches the setup exactly. ✓
  • Choice B — $v = 0.03t + 24$: Rate 0.03 mL/s would mean a full 0.03 mL drop every second, not every 3 seconds. ✗
  • Choice C — $v = 0.09t + 24$: Rate 0.09 mL/s is 9 times the actual rate. ✗
  • Choice D — $v = 3t$: No initial 24 mL, and the rate is wrong. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
